name: Best Ever French Bread
recipeIngredient: Vegetable cooking spray – for spraying pan
recipeInstructions:
The following is one of the best and easiest recipes I have ever come across for French bread. I use it as a basic recipe, embellish it, and create many different loaves now. As you will see, it is low in fat and sodium and absolutely wonderful.
1. In a large bowl combine shortening, sugar, and salt. Pour two cups boiling water over and mix; cool to lukewarm.
2. Dissolve 2 packages yeast in ½ cup warm water and 1 teaspoon sugar. Add to cooled liquid; stir in 6 ¼ cups flour all at once. It will appear as though there is too much flour at the start of the stirring process. Do not be alarmed. Just continue with your stirring process until flour is mixed.
3. Cover; allow to stand for 10 minutes. Stir again; cover; allow to stand for another 10 minutes. Continue this for a total of 1 hour.
4. Divide dough in half. On a floured surface, roll into a rectangle; roll up like a jelly roll. Place on a baking sheet sprayed with cooking spray and dusted with cornmeal.
5. Cut slashes in the top. Brush each with a mixture made of one egg white and 2 tablespoons water.
6. Cover and allow to rise until doubled, about 30 minutes.
7. Bake at 350°F for 25 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches your desired level (check by inserting a thermometer into the center). After 15 minutes in the oven, brush once more with the egg white mixture.
